mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals broad economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. This effect is always active regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is non-positive over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, the labor market is extremely tight, leading to wage inflation pressure and eventual monetary tightening that depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.5% over any 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts combined with a restrictive fed funds rate above 5% jointly signal a housing sector slowdown interacting with tight monetary policy, which depresses economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declining over 3 months and FEDFUNDS > 5%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing market weakness
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not produce an average negative 60-day SPX return in at least 60% of cases over any 5-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a low and stable inflation regime defined by CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth below 3%, equity risk premiums compress as uncertainty about Fed tightening recedes, supporting positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward SPX returns are negative on average or indistinguishable from zero when the CPI regime condition holds over any 2-year window, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth decelerates to below 2% annualized, it signals an economic slowdown that erodes corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If in instances of sub-2% GDP growth, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive or flat in at least 50% of cases over a 10-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) above 0.5% signals robust consumer spending, driving revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If strong retail sales are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than half of cases over any 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth exceeds 200k per month and the unemployment rate falls below 4.5% simultaneously, the labor market is overheating, which forces the Fed to tighten aggressively and crushes equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of occurrences over any 5-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle when the fed funds rate is above 5% and has been raised consecutively, any further increase in industrial production signals overheating that will be met with even tighter policy, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within the week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased at each of the last 3 FOMC meetings and > 5%
failure_condition: If in periods satisfying the trigger, the correlation between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward SPX return is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window, reject.
